cit.basis: Flat 15% CIT (Royal Decree No. 28/2009 as amended); concessionary 3% rate for qualifying small enterprises (registered capital <= OMR 50,000, <= 15 employees, gross income <= OMR 100,000, sector conditions).
cit.note: Administered by the Oman Tax Authority. Petroleum income is taxed at a special 55% rate under concession agreements — out of scope for this SME estimator.

wht.note: 10% withholding tax on dividends, interest, royalties and service fees paid to non-residents. In practice, WHT on dividends and interest has been suspended by Oman Tax Authority decision; royalties and service fees remain subject to WHT.

vat.note: 5% VAT effective April 2021 (Royal Decree No. 121/2020), with zero-rating and exemptions for specified supplies.

personal_income_tax.note: No personal income tax on employment income as of 2025. Oman approved a 5% personal income tax on high earners effective 2028 (Royal Decree No. 56/2025) — the first PIT in the GCC.

social_insurance.note: PASI/Social Protection Fund contributions apply to Omani employees only (7% employee, 10.5% employer, plus 1% job security); expatriates are not covered.

effective_date_range.basis: Target ruleset/params year; jurisdiction fiscal calendars and transitional periods may differ.

rate_threshold_assumptions: Rates and thresholds are simplified for LedgerField estimates.
SME qualification, petroleum-sector rates, WHT suspensions, relief, filing, and sector-specific rules may alter outcomes.
Verify against official guidance and local advisor before filing or production use.
limitations: Not legal, tax, accounting, or filing advice.
Does not encode every deduction, exemption, treaty, WHT, payroll, or filing rule.
High-stakes use requires local validation against official forms and current law.
